title: Extraction studies of PbII from salicylate media using neutral organophosphorous extractant, Cyanex-923 in toluene
author: S. M. Ghag
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Siddharth College of Arts, Science & Commerce, Dr. D. N. Road, Fort, 
Mumbai-400 001, India 
 E-mail  : sureshpawar_2004@rediffmail.com, ghag.snehal@yahoo.com 
 Manuscript received 22 May 2008, revised 8 July 2008, accepted 29 July 2008  

 The neutral extractant, Cyanex-923 has been used for the extraction of Pb II  from sodium salicylate media. 
This metal ion was found to be quantitatively extracted with Cyanex-923 in toluene in the pH range 6-7 and from the 
organic phase it is stripped with 1.5  M  HCl solution. The effect of pH, sodium salicylate concentration, reagent concentration, 
equilibration period, diluents, diverse ions and stripping agent on the extraction of Pb II  has been studied.
